svg.title: RAM layout: std::vector (contiguous) vs std::map/list (scattered)
svg.desc: Two side-by-side RAM diagrams. Left: std::vector with 6 floats packed into one cache line, shown in orange. Right: std::map/list with 6 nodes scattered across different cache lines, each requiring a separate RAM fetch.
svg.text: all 6 values in one cache line
1 RAM fetch → all 6 values in cache → ~4 cycles each
prefetcher engaged, entire array stays hot
6 RAM fetches → 1 value per fetch → up to ~200 cycles each
prefetcher blind — each pointer leads somewhere unknown
